steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word "thus" signals a logical consequence. The setup is: military service familiarizes veterans with organizational structures also found in civilian government. The blank must state what naturally follows from that familiarity — specifically, something that explains why veterans are overrepresented in civilian government jobs.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — makes civilian government jobs especially appealing to military veterans.: Familiarity with an organization's structure makes working within it more attractive. This follows directly from the premise and explains the overrepresentation of veterans in civilian government jobs. ✓
  • Choice B — alters the typical relationship between military service and subsequent career preferences.: The text never establishes what a "typical" relationship between military service and career preferences looks like, so there is no basis for claiming the relationship is being altered. ✗
  • Choice C — encourages nonveterans applying for civilian government jobs to consider military service instead.: The passage focuses on veterans moving into civilian government, not on nonveterans reconsidering military service. This reverses the direction of the argument. ✗
  • Choice D — increases the number of civilian government jobs that require some amount of military experience to perform.: The passage explains veterans' attraction to existing jobs due to shared structures, not that the jobs themselves are being changed to require military experience. ✗
A is correct.
